subject: Fermi GRB190512.61 Global MASTER-Net observations report
submitter: Vladimir Lipunov at Moscow State U/Krylov Obs  <lipunov@xray.sai.msu.ru>
body:
V. Lipunov, E. Gorbovskoy, V.Kornilov, D.Kuvshinov, N.Tyurina, P.Balanutsa,
A.Kuznetsov, V.V.Chazov, D. Vlasenko
(Lomonosov Moscow State University,
Sternberg Astronomical Institute, Moscow State University)

A. Tlatov, V.Senik, A.V. Parhomenko, D. Dormidontov
(Kislovodsk Solar Station of the Pulkovo Observatory)

K. Ivanov, O. Gres, N.M. Budnev, S. Yazev, O. Chuvalaev, V. Poleshchuk
(Irkutsk State University)

V. Yurkov, A. Gabovich, Yu. Sergienko
(Blagoveschensk Educational State University, Blagoveschensk)

R. Podesta, Carlos Lopez and F. Podesta
(Observatorio Astronomico Felix Aguilar (OAFA))

Hugo Levato and Carlos Saffe
(Instituto de Ciencias Astronomicas, de la Tierra y del Espacio (ICATE))

R. Rebolo, M. Serra, N. Lodieu, G. Israelian, L. Suarez-Andres
(The Instituto de Astrofisica de Canarias)

D. Buckley, S. Potter, A. Kniazev, M. Kotze
(South African Astronomical Observatory)



MASTER-SAAO robotic telescope  (Global MASTER-Net: http://observ.pereplet.ru, Lipunov et al., 2010, Advances in Astronomy, vol. 2010, 30L)  located in South Africa (South African Astronomical Observatory) started inspect of the Fermi GRB190512.61 (trigger No 579364804,  5h 21m 09.60s , -10d 39m 00.00s, R=2.98) errorbox  7144 sec after trigger time at 2019-05-12 16:39:03 UT, with upper limit up to  18.8 mag. Observations started at twilight.  The observations began at zenit distance = 59 deg. The sun  altitude  is -10.3 deg.

The galactic latitude b = -60 deg., longitude l = 98 deg.


Real time updated cover map and OT discovered available here:
https://master.sai.msu.ru/site/master2/observ.php?id=1016618

We obtain a following upper limits.
